questions.explanation: The module: 'TVET helps to provide varying job opportunities in almost every sphere of life ... These enable individuals to make ends meet by getting the necessities of life (food, clothing, shelter) and some luxuries to reduce the pressures on individuals and the government.' (Unit 4, Session 6)

units.title: Unit 5: Misconceptions About TVET Courses in Ghana

questions.question: What is a misconception?
options: A wrong notion or impression people have about an issue, a person, or an event
questions.explanation: The module: 'It is a wrong notion or impression people have about an issue, a person, or an event.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What is a misconception about Technical Skills?
options: Technical education is for dull people
Technical education is for bright people
Technical education is for women only
questions.explanation: The module: 'Technical Education is for the Dull People – Sadly, in this technological era, most Ghanaians are still living the wrong idea that technical education is meant for people who are not good academically.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Technical Skills?
options: Technical schools offer the same degrees as universities
questions.explanation: The module: 'Technical Schools offer inferior degrees. Many people think that degrees awarded by technical institutions are of lower value.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Technical Skills?
options: Job opportunities for technical students are limited
Job opportunities for technical students are abundant
Job opportunities are the same as for all graduates
Job opportunities are only in the public sector
questions.explanation: The module: 'Job Opportunities for technical students are limited. Some people have the wrong idea that Technical skills graduates are not well paid and have fewer job opportunities.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Technical Skills?
options: Technical Skill is a field for only men
Technical Skill is a field for only women
Technical Skill is a field for both genders equally
Technical Skill is not for anyone
questions.explanation: The module: 'Technical Skill is a Field for Only Men. Males used to dominate this industry probably because females are not good in mathematics, science, and technology.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Technical Skills?
options: Technical education is the same as general education
questions.explanation: The module: 'Technical Education is very easy. There is the wrong idea that Technical education is easy. This is wrong because Technical education takes learners through detailed core concepts which are very demanding.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Technical Skills?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Technical works are dirty. People have the wrong impression that you get dirty when you go to work.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What is a misconception about Visual Arts?
options: Visual Art is only about painting and drawing
Visual Art includes drama, music, dance, ceramics, graphic design
Visual Art is only about sculpture
Visual Art is only about photography
questions.explanation: The module: 'Visual Art is only about painting and drawing. Many people think Visual Arts people only paint and draw objects. This is a wrong idea because painting and drawing are just one aspect of Visual Arts. It consists of other aspects such as drama, music, dance, ceramics, graphic design, etc.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Visual Arts?
options: Visual Art is for lazy people
Visual Art is for hardworking people
Visual Art is only for artists
questions.explanation: The module: 'Visual Art is for Lazy People. There is a wrong perception that Visual Arts is for lazy people. Visual Art consists of so many theory courses which require students to have in-depth knowledge in the area besides the extremely practical ones.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Visual Arts?
options: Visual Art is for people with poor grades
Visual Art is for people with good grades
Visual Art is for people with average grades
Visual Art is for people with no grades
questions.explanation: The module: 'Visual Art is for people with poor grades. There is this wrong impression that Visual Arts is for those who are academically weak. It is rather the creative and fast thinkers who can become an artist.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Visual Arts?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Art Education is very costly. Arts Education is not as expensive as people complain. However, if even arts education is expensive, it is worth offering it because the returns and the monetary gains attached to it after one has completed will offset any debt incurred during the training.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Visual Arts?
options: Arts graduates have many job opportunities
questions.explanation: The module: 'Arts graduates are often jobless. This is also a misconception about Visual Art. There are a lot of job opportunities for Arts people.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is a misconception about Home Economics?
options: Home Economics is about cooking and sewing only
Home Economics is a broad field drawing from many disciplines
Home Economics is only about management
Home Economics is only about textiles
questions.explanation: The module: 'Home Economics is about Cooking and Sewing. Many people have the wrong impression that Home Economics is all about cooking and sewing, this is not true because cooking and sewing is just an aspect of Home Economics.' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Home Economics?
options: Home Economics is a female-related subject
Home Economics is for both genders
Home Economics is for males only
Home Economics is not for anyone
questions.explanation: The module: 'Home Economics is a female-related subject. There is this wrong impression that Home Economics is a field for only women. Though Home Economics will help you improve your home, its primary aim is to equip individuals to have a good life.' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Home Economics?
options: Home Economics is for intellectually disadvantaged people
Home Economics is for intellectually advantaged people
Home Economics is only for women
questions.explanation: The module: 'Home Economics is for Intellectually Disadvantaged People. Many people have the wrong impression that Home Economics is for people who are low achievers. This is not true because it is a unique field of study that has its own body of knowledge.' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Home Economics?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Home Economics is very costly. Home Economics is a practical course that comes with constant practice therefore some financial obligations are there. But the good thing about it is you can get your money back quickly by producing for sale.' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is a misconception about Agriculture Science?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Agriculture is a Dirty Job. People think agriculture is a dirty job because you cannot wear a suit to the farm or dress like an office worker.' (Unit 5, Session 4)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Agriculture Science?
options: Farming is for academically disadvantaged people
Farming is for academically advantaged people
Farming is only for rural people
questions.explanation: The module: 'Farming is for academically disadvantaged people. Most people think is good for non-academics. This is not true because Knowledge and practice of agriculture which farming is just an aspect is derived from the pure sciences i.e. Biology, Chemistry, and Physics.' (Unit 5, Session 4)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Agriculture Science?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Farming is an outdated occupation. People see the agriculture sector as living in the past thus the use of rudimentary tools e.g. cutlass, hoe, etc. There are very sophisticated tools and equipment in the agriculture sector in the Western world.' (Unit 5, Session 4)

questions.question: What is another misconception about Agriculture Science?
options: People in agriculture destroy the environment
People in agriculture protect the environment
People in agriculture have no impact on the environment
People in agriculture only benefit the environment
questions.explanation: The module: 'People in Agriculture destroy the environment. People believe that agriculture harms the environment. This is a wrong notion because it rather enhances the capacity of individuals to adopt practices that are environmentally friendly to reduce environmental degradation.' (Unit 5, Session 4)

questions.question: What is one way to address misconceptions about TVET?
options: Education – refocus education so people learn to do something with knowledge after school
Only teach TVET at higher levels
questions.explanation: The module: 'Education: Education in the country should be refocused so that people, will not only learn just to read, and write but do not know what to do with the knowledge acquired after school. ... Students at the early grade thus at the Basic level of education should be taught to embrace TVET.' (Unit 5, Session 5)

questions.question: What is another way to address misconceptions about TVET?
options: Motivation – generate and sustain students' interest in TVET at early stages and invite successful TVET graduates to talk to learners
questions.explanation: The module: 'Motivation: Teaching and learning must generate and sustain students’ interest in TVET at the early stages of education and give good guidance to them... Successful resource persons who made it through TVET must be invited often to talk to learners to build their confidence.' (Unit 5, Session 5)

questions.question: What is another way to address misconceptions about TVET?
options: Government policies – abolish compulsory admission of low-grade students into TVET, revamp and equip vocational schools, educate the public
questions.explanation: The module: 'Government policies: Policymakers, educators, and stakeholders must realize that the old system of education ... must give way to the current trend of education which places more emphasis on the acquisition of practical skills. Government must institute policies that will help revive and improve the TVET sector.' (Unit 5, Session 5)

questions.question: What is another way to address misconceptions about TVET?
options: Encouragement of practical project work for students – so they discover the benefits of TVET themselves
questions.explanation: The module: 'Encouragement of Practical Project Work for Students: Students should be made to do practical projects in their area of interest as part of their education process before graduation. This will give the chance for them to discover the benefits of TVET themselves.' (Unit 5, Session 5)

questions.question: What is another way to address misconceptions about TVET?
options: Positive teacher attitude to TVET – teachers must understand TVET and have a good attitude towards it
Only train teachers in academic subjects
questions.explanation: The module: 'Positive Teacher Attitude to TVET: Teachers must endeavour to understand TVET better and have a good attitude toward it as other subjects and encourage learners to go into it.' (Unit 5, Session 5)

questions.question: What career opportunity is related to TVET?
questions.explanation: The module lists many careers: 'Building engineers, Mechanical engineers, Dressmaking and Tailoring, Interior and Exterior decoration, Nutritionist, Fashion designer, Painter, Sculptor, Carpenters, Agribusiness Person, Electrical Engineers, etc.' (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is a building engineer?
options: An expert in the use of technology for the design, construction, assessment, and maintenance of the built environment
A person who only designs buildings
A person who only constructs buildings
A person who only maintains buildings
questions.explanation: The module: 'A Building Engineer is recognized as being an expert in the use of technology for the design, construction, assessment, and maintenance of the built environment.' (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is a mechanical engineer?
options: A person who designs power-producing machines, such as electric generators, internal combustion engines, and steam turbines
A person who only repairs cars
A person who only designs buildings
A person who only works with electricity
questions.explanation: The module: 'Mechanical engineers design power-producing machines, such as electric generators, internal combustion engines, and steam and gas turbines, as well as power-using machines, such as refrigeration and air-conditioning systems.' (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is a nutritionist?
options: An expert in food and nutrition who advises people on healthy eating practices to prevent diseases
A person who only cooks food
A person who only sells food
A person who only studies food
questions.explanation: The module: 'A nutritionist is an expert in food and nutrition who advises people on healthy eating practices to prevent diseases.' (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is a fashion designer?
options: A person who studies fashion trends, sketches designs, selects colours and fabrics, and oversees final production
A person who only sews clothes
A person who only sells clothes
A person who only wears clothes
questions.explanation: The module: 'A fashion designer studies fashion trends, sketch designs of clothing and accessories, select colours and fabrics and oversee the final production of the design.' (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is a painter (artist)?
options: A person who produces original artworks using a variety of media, usually on a flat surface
A person who only paints houses
A person who only paints cars
A person who only paints signs
questions.explanation: The module: 'Painters produce original artworks using a variety of media such as watercolour, oil, acrylics, pastels, crayon, pencil, or charcoal usually on a flat surface.' (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is a sculptor?
options: A person who designs three-dimensional artworks using methods like modelling, carving, casting, constructing, and assembling
A person who only carves wood
A person who only moulds clay
A person who only casts metal
questions.explanation: The module: 'Sculptors design three or two-dimensional artworks, by using methods such as modelling, carving, casting, constructing, and assembling with materials such as clay, glass, wire, plastic, fabric, or metal, wood, stone, etc.' (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is a carpenter?
options: A person who works with wood, making cabinets, building houses, or doing other things with wood
A person who only builds houses
A person who only makes cabinets
A person who only repairs furniture
questions.explanation: The module: 'A carpenter is a person who works with wood. They can make cabinets, build houses, or do other things with wood.' (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is an agribusiness person?
options: A person involved in all steps required to send agricultural goods to the market, including production, processing, and distribution
A person who only processes goods
A person who only distributes goods
questions.explanation: The module: 'Agribusiness Person: The business involves all the steps required to send agricultural goods to the market which include production, processing, and distribution.' (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is an electrical engineer?
options: A person who designs and develops new electrical systems, solves problems, and tests equipment
A person who only installs wiring
A person who only repairs appliances
A person who only works with power lines
questions.explanation: The module: 'An electrical engineer is someone who designs and develops new electrical systems, solves problems, and tests equipment.' (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is another career opportunity in TVET?
questions.explanation: The module lists: 'Interior and Exterior decoration' as a career. (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is another career opportunity in TVET?
questions.explanation: The module lists: 'Dressmaking and Tailoring' as a career. (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is the role of education in addressing TVET misconceptions?
options: To refocus education so students learn practical skills and embrace TVET from basic school
To only teach TVET at university
questions.explanation: The module: 'Education in the country should be refocused so that people, will not only learn just to read, and write but do not know what to do with the knowledge acquired after school.' (Unit 5, Session 5)

questions.question: What is the role of motivation in addressing TVET misconceptions?
options: To generate and sustain students' interest in TVET through guidance and role models
To only focus on academic subjects
questions.explanation: The module: 'Teaching and learning must generate and sustain students’ interest in TVET at the early stages of education and give good guidance to them.' (Unit 5, Session 5)

questions.question: What is the role of government policies in addressing TVET misconceptions?
options: To institute policies that revive and improve the TVET sector, such as abolishing compulsory admission of low-grade students, revamping schools, and educating the public
To only focus on academic schools
questions.explanation: The module: 'Government must institute policies that will help revive and improve the TVET sector such as abolishing the compulsory admission of students with very low grades into TVET programs, revamping and equipping Vocational and Technical schools, helping in educating and sensitizing the public on TVET.' (Unit 5, Session 5)

questions.question: What is the role of practical project work in addressing TVET misconceptions?
options: To give students real-life experiences to discover the benefits of TVET themselves
questions.explanation: The module: 'Students should be made to do practical projects in their area of interest as part of their education process before graduation. This will give the chance for them to discover the benefits of TVET themselves.' (Unit 5, Session 5)

questions.question: What is the role of teacher attitude in addressing TVET misconceptions?
options: Teachers must have a positive attitude towards TVET and encourage learners to pursue it
Teachers should only teach academic subjects
Teachers should discourage students from TVET
questions.explanation: The module: 'Teachers must endeavour to understand TVET better and have a good attitude toward it as other subjects and encourage learners to go into it.' (Unit 5, Session 5)

questions.question: What is one TVET-related career opportunity?
questions.explanation: The module lists 'Fashion designer' as a TVET career. (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is one TVET-related career opportunity?
questions.explanation: The module lists 'Sculptor' as a TVET career. (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is one TVET-related career opportunity?
questions.explanation: The module lists 'Carpenters' as a TVET career. (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is one TVET-related career opportunity?
questions.explanation: The module lists 'Painter' as a TVET career. (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is one TVET-related career opportunity?
questions.explanation: The module lists 'Agribusiness Person' as a TVET career. (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is one TVET-related career opportunity?
questions.explanation: The module lists 'Electrical Engineers' as a TVET career. (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is one TVET-related career opportunity?
questions.explanation: The module lists 'Mechanical engineers' as a TVET career. (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is one TVET-related career opportunity?
questions.explanation: The module lists 'Building engineers' as a TVET career. (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is one TVET-related career opportunity?
questions.explanation: The module lists 'Nutritionist' as a TVET career. (Unit 5, Session 6)

questions.question: What is technology?
options: The application of scientific knowledge for practical purposes, especially in industry
questions.explanation: The module: 'Technology is the application of scientific knowledge for practical purposes, especially in industry.' (Unit 6, Session 1)

questions.question: What does technology involve?
options: Machinery and equipment developed from the application of scientific knowledge
questions.explanation: The module: 'It involves machinery and equipment developed from the application of scientific knowledge.' (Unit 6, Session 1)

questions.question: What is an example of technology in TVET?
questions.explanation: The module lists: 'Wood technology, Metal technology, Building construction technology, Electronic Technology, Electrical technology, Automotive technology.' (Unit 6, Session 1)

questions.question: What is Building construction technology?
options: A trade used to create, design, and erect structural components, including building houses and skyscrapers
questions.explanation: The module: 'Building construction technology: It is a trade used to create, design, and erect structural, and make and install their various components. This includes the act of building houses, skyscrapers.' (Unit 6, Session 1)

questions.question: How does technology improve communications?
options: Companies rely on applications and software for communicating with others, such as phone services, video conferencing, Skype, and instant messaging
questions.explanation: The module: 'Companies rely on different applications and software for communicating with others. ... phone services provide quality voice... Other ways of communication include video conferencing, Skype, and instant messaging.' (Unit 6, Session 2)

questions.question: How does technology improve efficiency?
options: Technology increases the efficiency of the company through robotics and applied sciences, saving time and effort
questions.explanation: The module: 'Technology increases the efficiency of the company. The advancements in the world of robotics and applied sciences help businesses... saving time and effort involved in employing human labour and increases productivity.' (Unit 6, Session 2)

questions.question: How does technology protect against attacks?
options: Developers design cyber security such as antivirus systems to protect companies from dangerous threats
questions.explanation: The module: 'developers are designing cyber security such as antivirus systems to protect companies from dangerous threats.' (Unit 6, Session 2)

questions.question: How does technology help workers work efficiently?
options: Employees expect supervisors to provide the latest equipment to work effectively and efficiently
questions.explanation: The module: 'Employees also expect their supervisors to provide them with the latest equipment so that they can work effectively as well as efficiently.' (Unit 6, Session 2)

questions.question: How does technology add value to teaching and learning in TVET?
options: It increases access for marginalized groups, helps in re-skilling workers through distance education, and provides a climate for growth
questions.explanation: The module: 'It increases access for people from marginalized and disadvantaged groups. For example, the use of phones and computers for distance education programs. ICT helps in re-skilling workers in the job market through distance education learning.' (Unit 6, Session 2)

questions.question: What is decision-making?
options: A choice or judgment that one or individuals have to make after thinking about the best thing to do
The process of making random choices
questions.explanation: The module: 'It is simply a choice or judgment that one or individuals have to make after thinking and talking about what is the best thing to do.' (Unit 6, Session 3)

questions.question: What are core values?
options: Those values we hold that form the foundation of our individual lives and on which we perform our work and conduct ourselves
questions.explanation: The module: 'Core values are those values we hold that form the foundation of our individual lives and on which we perform our work and conduct ourselves.' (Unit 6, Session 3)

questions.question: What are core competencies?
options: The harmonized combination of multiple resources and skills that distinguish an individual's performance from others
questions.explanation: The module: 'Core competencies are the harmonized combination of multiple resources and skills that distinguish an individual’s performance from the others.' (Unit 6, Session 3)

questions.question: What is the relevance of decision-making, core values, and core competencies in TVET?
options: They help identify community problems, find solutions, inform thoughts and actions, guide career choices, and save time
They only help in the workplace
They only help in personal life
questions.explanation: The module: 'Decision Making, Core Values, and Core Competencies help to identify community problems and find solutions to them. They inform our thoughts, decisions, and actions and help us to choose our careers or life path... They help to save time and make better use of resources at our workplace.' (Unit 6, Session 3)

questions.question: What is the relevance of proper value systems at the workplace?
options: Values like honesty, hard work, dedication, commitment, punctuality, concern for others, and tolerance sustain both the work and employees
questions.explanation: The module discusses values like 'honesty, hard work, dedication, commitment, punctuality, etc.' and their relevance to sustaining the organization. (Unit 6, Session 4)

questions.question: What is honesty in the workplace?
options: Being faithful; things can be entrusted to honest people and they render proper accounts
questions.explanation: The module: 'Honest people are faithful. Things can be entrusted into their care and they can render proper accounts to their superiors.' (Unit 6, Session 4)

questions.question: What is hard work in the workplace?
options: Being an asset to oneself and the organization, helping achieve targets, making profit, and being self-motivated
questions.explanation: The module: 'A hard-working person is an asset to himself or herself and society and the organization. ... They can make a profit to sustain the organization and the employees. ... Hard-working people are self-motivated.' (Unit 6, Session 4)

questions.question: What is dedication in the workplace?
options: Being devoted to the task, working wholeheartedly, and ensuring success
questions.explanation: The module: 'Dedicated people are devoted. They are devoted to their task. They work their hearts out because they love their work and are willing to ensure its success.' (Unit 6, Session 4)

questions.question: What is punctuality in the workplace?
options: Being ready to work on time, increasing productivity and profits
Only coming to work on time
questions.explanation: The module: 'A punctual worker will get ready to work on time. When workers develop that habit of punctuality, they can increase their productivity and subsequently profits margin of the organization will also go up.' (Unit 6, Session 4)

questions.question: What is commitment in the workplace?
options: Seeing the job as something that deserves all attention, making sacrifices to achieve targets
questions.explanation: The module: 'A committed person sees his or her job as something that deserves all his or her attention. He or she does not allow anything to stand between himself and his work. ... he or she is prepared to make sacrifices necessary to work until the set target is achieved.' (Unit 6, Session 4)

questions.question: What is concern for others in the workplace?
options: Extending a hand of support to others, especially those we work with
questions.explanation: The module: 'We need to extend a hand of support to others, especially to the people we come into contact and work with.' (Unit 6, Session 4)

questions.question: What is tolerance in the workplace?
options: Respecting people's opinions or behaviors, especially those you may not agree with
questions.explanation: The module: 'Tolerance means being able to respect people’s opinions or behaviors especially those you may not agree with. It may also mean living with people who may not behave or think like you.' (Unit 6, Session 4)

questions.question: What is entrepreneurship?
options: The process by which individuals pursue opportunities without regard to resources they currently control
The process of starting a business without risk
The process of working for someone else
questions.explanation: The module: 'Entrepreneurship: It is the process by which individuals pursue opportunities without regard to resources they currently control. (Barringer and Ireland,2008)' (Unit 6, Session 5)

questions.question: Who is an entrepreneur?
options: The person who organizes the other factors of production (land, labour, and capital) to set up a business to make a profit
A person who only invests money
A person who only manages a business
A person who only owns a business
questions.explanation: The module: 'He/she is the person who organizes the other factors of production namely land, labour, and capital to set up a business to make a profit.' (Unit 6, Session 5)

questions.question: What are qualities of an entrepreneur?

questions.question: What are employability skills?
options: The transferable skills needed by individuals to make them 'employable', including personal qualities and values
questions.explanation: The module: 'Employability skills can be defined as the transferable skills needed by individuals to make them ‘employable’. ... They are the essential skills, personal qualities, and values that enable you to thrive in any workplace.' (Unit 6, Session 6)

questions.question: What is one employability skill?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Personal initiative and being self-motivated: Being creative and having new ideas of your own and bringing them on board for the development of your workplace.' (Unit 6, Session 6)

questions.question: What is another employability skill?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Ability to learn and adapt: To be able to acquire new skills and knowledge in new situations and learn from your mistakes and others.' (Unit 6, Session 6)

questions.question: What is another employability skill?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Problem-Solving Skills: The ability to perceive and understand a problem by breaking it down into smaller constituents, identifying key issues and solutions.' (Unit 6, Session 6)

questions.question: What is another employability skill?
options: Working under pressure and meeting deadlines
questions.explanation: The module: 'Working Under Pressure and Meeting Deadlines: The ability to work and meet deadlines under stressful conditions.' (Unit 6, Session 6)

questions.question: What is another employability skill?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Organizational Skills: The ability to plan and be methodical in monitoring, and meeting targets and deadlines.' (Unit 6, Session 6)

questions.question: What is another employability skill?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Team working: Working together with other people from different backgrounds, disciplines, and expertise to meet goals.' (Unit 6, Session 6)

questions.question: What is another employability skill?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Appreciating unity in diversity: Knowing the value of diversity and what it can bring and understanding and being considerate of the different needs of different individuals.' (Unit 6, Session 6)

questions.question: What is another employability skill?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Negotiation Skills: The ability to know what you want to achieve and take into account other people’s feelings without attaching emotions to it and getting to a good conclusion on what you both need.' (Unit 6, Session 6)

questions.question: What is another employability skill?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Confidence: Believing in yourself, your colleagues, and the institution or company you work for without being arrogant.' (Unit 6, Session 6)

questions.question: What is another employability skill?
questions.explanation: The module: 'Communication Skills: The ability to explain what you mean clearly and concisely (oral, written, presenting, listening) enables you to pass on the right information without distortion.' (Unit 6, Session 6)
